transcript: respondents cast total 10 votes. 5 of them voted for third step of the ladder. Which indicates females of this village have Power & freedom to make some major life decisions.
now days we are educated, trained and follow mass media. We make some major decisions on our life and I put myself on step 3.
Similarly Iĺm on transitional phase of my life because my livelihood is getting improved with more production and education over time.

I have plan to marry and have children. Before that I need to have property first to raise my children. I do not want them to grow by chance as the saying goes ôBeedilu Yadgalĺ literally translated as ôa child will grow by his/her own chanceö. All these things are my decision and therefore I am on step 4.

in many cases our country is in the process of development. We are also developing with the new developments we are using improved seeds and diversifying our livelihoods. As we grow and have more opportunities the chance to make decision is growing so now we are at step 3 on our way towards steps 4 and 5.

we also have to consider the limited resource and financial constraint we face as young people to become economically well and get the ultimate power and freedom to decide. For me we are even less than three as we still are under our parents.

education is very important to live and even we need education for farming and to adopt new technologies.  It is also important to reach our aspiration to be future engineers, doctors ů and become productive citizen.

itĺs appropriate and every person has to learn because if we do not learn we will be illiterate and have limited access to job opportunities. It is obvious that uneducated person stays at home whereas educated one becomes a doctor.  Even they have the opportunity to go to America.

it is essential even for family planning, as we see that those who are educated families have limited family size unlike the uneducated ones.  Family planning is an essential issue as we have limited land.
in our kebele, many of the boys study until 8th or 9th grade and get married.
many of them drop out from 8th grade considering the cost of accommodation as the high school (9-12) is located in XX.
boys drop out because of economic problem of their parents and have to support their families at the farm land.
in case of girls they study until grade 6th and are forced to get married because they are considered as grownups by this time.

uneducated parents may not send their girls to school to support them on household chores Previously. However, currently, parents are aware of the importance of education and send their girls to school.
it is appropriate for youths to study up to college and university level in order to improve their living status because they have the opportunity to be hired or become entrepreneurs.
itĺs the same for girls to study up to college and university to improve their social and economic opportunities. This is linked to later marriage and self employment.
Many young boys who are employed want to marry employed girls. So girls should study.
these days a number of girls studied up to 10th grade and they are still unemployed.
you canĺt find uneducated young girls at this kebele under 30 years of age now days because the parents acknowledged the advantage of education and send both sons and daughters to school.
The schools (1-8) have been constructed in every kebele then access and distance is not a problem for primary education and junior high schools.

previously, parents donĺt even sent their sons to school. However, they know that educated person can have better opportunity and improve his living status. As a result, currently parents are showing better commitment to send their children both girls and boys to school.
boys as young as 15 support his father at the farm in the meantime, learning how to plow, rare cattle and work with hoe.
he learns about different varieties of seeds and how to weed including different strategies of farming such as planting wheat once and not to repeat the same seed for the next planting period.
unlike boys girls stays with their mother at their early age and learn mainly household chores and may participate in farming when there is a need for extra labor, such as harvesting time.
girls donĺt mainly involve in agricultural tasks because it is believed that agriculture related tasks are for the man and requires power.
Reasons for girls leaving education are:
cannot access the  high school tha is far away in a different place
cannot go to school in case of labor support on the family farm plot
Economic problem of parents
Sickness
Lack of electricity (to study at night)
Family separation
Low farm production - means less money to spend on school
Early marriage
Two most important reasons for girls leaving education are:
Economic problem of parents
Family separation, when this happens the chances of being sent to school are less

young boys drop out of school because they canĺt afford accommodation to study at XX , for instance I have studied up to 8th grade but I canĺt continue to 9th grade as a result of shortage of money to cover house rent and food.

Natural  factor which is unusual distribution of rainfall (heavy rain) to some extent contribute to the decline in crop production and that affects our livelihoods such reasons creates economic problem in the family which in turn affect the young people in the household.

Family separation has impact. As the family becomes female headed household, the burden of farming and other labor requiring tasks will rest on the shoulder of the older son. When the boy take over his fatherĺs responsibility, then he would not have no chance to continue school.
I have an ambition to be a teacher because I have been taught by a teacher and want to teach young children to open their eyes.
I want to be a doctor since everyone can work and live actively if healthy.
I want to have my own business so that I can hire others to work for me and also improve their livelihoods.
I would like to become an accountant. I like math.
I would like to become a farmer who works with machineries such as tractor and combine harvesters.
not many of them graduated and came back. However, there are youths who are back to agriculture and others are employed as teachers and there are also others who work in organization in other cities.
Once they stop studying, many boys come back to their familyĺs land and are engaged in farming they donĺt have another option or opportunity in the area.
after they finished college or university, they have to take active role to create jobs rather than expecting to be recruited by government institution.

joining university or collage is not the only option for youths but they can join vocational trainings center and after they study they borrow money and organize into associations to be self employed.  I know there is vocational training center at XX.
Improved seeds
Planting eucalyptus trees
Feeding Trainings on row planting
improved milk cow at home
Backyard vegetables (beat root, carrot, cabbage)
Row planting (potato, wheat and maize )
Fertilizer (UREA and DAP)
Irrigation ( in very limited area)

the manĺs emphasis is to get income out of improved seeds production and he uses the money to support his family, buy cloth, send his children to school. Itĺs clear that he will buy fertilizer with the money.

while woman give more attention to feed the household then they give priority to improved milk cow and backyard vegetables. At the same time these products are means of income for women from selling butter and buy crops for consumption.
of course, they have equal opportunity and women can participate on trainings because when there are training opportunities it is not men specific it is available for both.
the law empowers women and they can participate in meetings but they donĺt have the capacity to face challenges as men.

As for the culture, in our kebele women do not inherent land unless their husband dies, This cultural practice is still going on now even if the law empower them. Thus, large amount land is dominated by men.
Women usually support the man in agriculture, and in the mean time learn from him about agriculture.  In the absence of land why would she need o learn about agriculture?
as indicates earlier, agriculture is the main task for men as a result agricultural development workers visits men while they go to farm plots and inform or teach who is available at the plot.
The woman is busy with household chores to take care of the man and the children.
Important crops or livestock forwomen
Maize
Wheat
Potato
Small livestock (sheep and goats)
Teff
Backyard vegetables
Planting tres
there is not much change in agricultural practices but planting tree and backyard vegetables are adopted more recently.

yes, we follow the tradition; woman alongside with household chores they mainly participate in backyard vegetables and look after the cattle and small livestock. In the farm plot they do participate on cutting, hoe and collecting but they donĺt farm with oxen.
with regard to backyard vegetables, men have started participating in growing backyard vegetables after they have been trained on improved crops for market by agricultural development workers.
In case of selling, it determined on the portion of production to sell. If itĺs in bulk the men take the responsibility.
currently, they do through consultation and still determined by the man in selling but she can sell small amount to local markets.
the man has the overall power on maize or the other crops. If she sells without his permission, she will be in trouble and an be beaten by the man.
there is a problem! How come a man milking and sell butter at the local market. Itĺs not a tradition for a man to sell these items as buyers are also women.
itĺs not the tradition and customary for a man to sell butter at the local market but no obstacle for the woman.

There is no as such divided land. The land belongs to the man. It is customary therefore, for the man to take care of the land and the woman to support him in the farm and take care of the household chores.
The women usually manage the household chores and backyard vegetables. If there is a need for labor and power the man takes over.
traditionally women donĺt plow if a man is in the house.

In terms of labor division the man is responsible to assign the children and the division determine by the age, power and demand. Practically, older children at the age of 13 go with their father to support on farming and the child learns to plow and weed and at earlier age.
there is no separated land, the woman helps her man in the farm in planting, weeding and harvesting seasons and he may not support her on backyard vegetables unless itĺs large.
Usually the man will work on the vegetable farm if the land is large. Otherwise, he is not helping.
if the family has girl child she will work with her mother and help her on household chores in case of older boys they go to the farm land to support their father or look after cattle.
in case of the woman, she has to consult her husband and children if they are grown up.
it is the same to the man; he also consults the wife and children. Especially he discuss with his wife.
he directly goes to the agricultural development workers if he has two wives and they donĺt agree with each other.
he wonĺt let her to join even if she is the one who grows the vegetables.
itĺs obvious that he doesnĺt allow her to join the group but she has to tell him and impress him about the benefit.
If Ana were to ask to sell her vegetables herself, Adam would respond ôwhat a surprising thing you learned?ö
He would not accept her idea because he should be the one who controls the income. Therefore, he wonĺt let her sell as he loses control of the money.
he may get angry with the idea and argue with her. Finally if she doesnĺt accept what he is saying he will beat her.
no, he doesnĺt even recognize that she is working exhaustively; he only focused on the income and meeting the labor demand.
she should support him because what he is doing is labor demanding, how would he manage it without her help?
it is not common for the man to stay in the house and look after the household chores and children while the woman goes to the market. They community label her as disobedient, and him as crazy.
they said she is a thief. She is robbing him off what is supposed to be his.
They will say, is he ok? Is he feeling normal? What is going on with him?
he is a kind of foolish and unwise person
label him as lazy one and is a ôwomanö who canĺt decide over his life.
he is like a property not a man.
if they agreed at the beginning, he will be happy with her accomplishment
he doesnĺt complain if she is successful even she didnĺt consult him at the initial stage.
itĺs about working and participating together without neglecting woman from opportunities for better development and improvement.
encourage girls to go to school not only educating boys and empower girls to attend school regularly.

equal opportunity in terms of education, skill trainings, inheritance of other property but not land because the portion of land is small and the woman may leave the house if she has the land and she will give it to the husband.[the respondents shows their support to the idea by laughing]
early times, men were the only ones attending meetings but currently both have equally attending and work together using their skills. Then equality is important to improve their life.

I have same perception about equality at this time women learn agricultural skill and health issues from agricultural and health extension agents equally with men. Therefore, both can work together for change.
much is done through consultation with each other whereas she would not decide alone on the amount.
the amount for market and consumption is set by both based on agreement. No one decides without the consent of the other.
if they do not agree, he should make an adjustment for mutual understanding or pass his decision and impose it on her to accept.

most likely, she would sell the small portion of backyard vegetables at the local market but he involved or takes control if the portion exceeds a basket full and he is the one who process the sell in to large markets because itĺs a challenge for her to transport the items in cart and vehicle.
if the production is in large quantity he takes the responsibility of selling in the large market because men are better than women on calculation of price.

most of the time, children are closer to their mother. If she has to travel, the children will uffer as a result, she would stay at home and takes care of her children instead of going to the market while her husband can travel and sale the vegetables.

at first, she has to tell him about the new practice and tell him about how her sister benefited from it then they will adopt if he agrees otherwise she has to take him to her sisterĺs farm and show him the new practice and the yields.
if the case is reversed, hemay consult her but it would not take her consent to adopt the practice. However, she has to convince him.

She belongs to him she is his asset and therefore, whatever she brings to the household is also his asset. He is the one who decides not her.

she should consult him and both must agree on what to do with the money. Practically she would not spend the money without telling him because it causes conflict among them and may lead to physical violence.
itĺs not easy or difficult for him that he has to consult her and recommend enough reason for her to buy the bike otherwise he canĺt pick the money and go to the market to buy the motorbike.
tĺs easy for him to buy since the money belongs to him and he is the husband.

it is not that easy to buy the bike immediately without consulting his wife.  ĹAheya yelilew ferese yigzalĺ, literally means, ôWho does not own a donkey would buy horseö . I mean it is like buying unessential material instead of giving priority for the need of the family. The same thing will happen if he doesnĺt consult with his wife and know his familyĺs need.
if he buys without telling her, they could fight that she keeps raising the issue and would separate then itĺs better for her to accept it easily and go on as not a big deal.
if he buys without telling her, she would send relatives, closer friends and elders to him to stop him buying it if he tells her before hand.

No, they are scared and not the tradition that women travel alone. [why are they scared?] they are scared because their parents would not allow them to go out by themselves. They are never used to getting out alone.
women donĺt usually travel alone scared of rape and their husband donĺt allow them to walk alone.
itĺs not positively perceived by the community if a woman travels alone (labeled as mentally unstable),
the women probably be cheated by a boy and rarely abducted if they travel unaccompanied
no one will touch her, because they are afraid of the consequence if they face her husband and in respect of her husband.
she is free from any danger in respect of her husband
she would not be positively seen by the community if she has a husband, family and expected to stay at home taking care of her family. As married or not have to stay at home.

easy for her, we have local market in the kebele and both men and women are selling and buying during day time but late at night it would be difficult for her and she has to reach home to serve her man. Otherwise she would be beaten f she comes late to home by her husband.
it is determined on the distance of the market because she should reach home early to be safe from rape and violence from her husband.

itĺs widely publicized about women right and freedom of movement but still there is obstacle on the traditional view of women in the area. They are educated women but only 3 women can move freely out of ten.
there is tension among women so they are not moving freely.
I know there are youths who went to XX and work there small roads construction as daily labors. It is matter of self motivation and money, if you have the two things itĺs easy.
you need to have family support

it depends on the status of the person who returned back. If they manage to improve their life when they come back, they will be referenced as models by parents. If not, they often turn out ot be thieves and addicts.

What opportunity there is for any one here except being a farmer? They may come but still they have little opportunity to engage into anything meaningful. Their father is a farmer and they will end up being a farmer.
for a girl, she gets married at the age between 17 to22 and for boys form age 22to 30. .
at the age of 15, the girl is considered young and not ready for marriage. In this case they wait until the age of 17.
in the case of boys, they wait until the age of 22 as they have to own property because he is expected to manage the family financially but girls are not expected to own property.
early times, parents settle marriage for their children though currently young people manage their own union.
it is a matter of chance because these days either young man married on their will or choice of his/her father or he/she choses his wife and her husband.
at this time, parents really donĺt want us [men] to marry unless we donĺt have property in case of gift of land and ĹGeberaĺ (bridal gift) to give to their daughter.

When it comes to co-wives, usually the man will chose and will inform the wife. Then if she agrees he will bring the new wife home if not he will make her stay somewhere until she is pregnant and he will bring her home. Later he would divide his land and property.
both should select their marriage partners and decide by themselves to marry.
women here have their first child between the ages of 18 and 20
yes, itĺs as she is old enough to take care of her baby.

no, itĺs not the right age; she will be more skilled in child care and life at the age of 22. And if she starts giving birth at the age of 20 imagine how many children she will have at the age of 40-50. In this case, 22 is the best age to have a baby.
it is preferable at the age of 21 and 22 because itĺs the appropriate age since she will have skill on how to take care of the baby.
woman has to give birth at the early of her twentyĺs because those children from older women (above 22) will be mentally unstable.
no, both women and men donĺt have plan when and how many children to have. They immediately gave birth after marriage.

in this case I want to see better working opportunities for youths created either by the government or non government institution to support us and at the same time fill the resources gap for work opportunities.
I want to see intervention for woman. Job opportunities on wood work, improved livestock and other related job opportunities that fit to the woman and see them employed.
